text.title: Company Prompt Library Pattern Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every company prompt-library spec must enforce. HARD rules — overrideable only via spec amendment, not via PR comment.

rule.statement: The library is partitioned by a named namespace map (e.g., `prompts/role/pm/`, `prompts/task/code-review/`). Free-floating files at `prompts/*.md` without a namespace slot are rejected.
rule.rationale: Without a namespace, the library degrades into a shared clipboard within 90 days; namespaces force authors to declare the consuming role/task and make retrieval deterministic.

rule.statement: The spec defines exactly four layers in this resolution order: faion-defaults → role-pack → company-override → repo-override. Each prompt declares which layer wins, and the agent loads them in that order.
rule.rationale: Unlayered overrides cause drift between teams and silent regression when faion defaults change; explicit layering preserves upstream improvements while letting the company override locally.

rule.statement: Every prompt PR runs an eval gate before merge: ≥1 rubric-based judge scorer + ≥1 deterministic check (regex / JSON-Schema / contract). PRs without a green gate are blocked.
rule.rationale: Prompts without a gate become opinion; the gate produces a falsifiable signal that lets reviewers approve based on metric delta, not vibes.

rule.statement: Each prompt file carries `version` (semver) and `last_reviewed` (ISO date) frontmatter. Prompts > 90 days without review must be flagged on load.
rule.rationale: Frozen prompts decay against model upgrades; mandatory review-by-date forces refresh cadence and makes staleness auditable.

rule.statement: Each role-pack and each company-override file names a single accountable owner (handle or email). No 'team' / 'we' as owner. Orphaned packs (owner left org) trigger a CI failure until reassigned.
rule.rationale: Plural ownership = no ownership; orphans rot. A named human is the only sustainable signal for who to chase when a prompt regression ships.
